titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John for the Byzantine Text Project
Cyril of Jerusalem
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: Roderic L. Mullen
and members of the International Greek New Testament Project

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Available for noncommercial re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators and the subsequent derivative is licensed as ShareAlike (Creative Commons by-nc-sa)

msIdentifier.msName: Cyril of Jerusalem (text reconstructed from citations)

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by Roderic Mullen and colleagues on the International Greek New Testament Project working towards an Electronic Edition of the Gospel According to John in the Byzantine Tradition, first published in 2007.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. Further information about the transcription procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Hugh Houghton and Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

The original plain text header reads as follows: Status:Cyril of Jerusalem --from RLM's book, which see for further details John begins Folio Transcribed by: RLMTranscription begun: 1992Transcription finished: 1993Transference to Collate by: RLMTransference to Collate begun: 15.07.03Transference to Collate finished: 15.07.03File date and time:Placed in Level1: RLMLevel1 file date and time: 15.07.03Placed in Level2:Placed in Level2 by: RLM-CJSLevel2 file date and time: 21.07.03These files were compared and differences resolved by:Placed in Level3:Placed in Level3 by:Final transcription file date and time: note-- The remark (allusion) means that all or part of a verse is attested only by an allusion. The Greek is given in parts of the verse where clear textual info is discernable.

revisionDesc.change: Converted from plain text by Hugh Houghton using Python conversion scripts 1.0.

div.ab: (not quoted) ιδων(not quoted) κατακειμενον(not quoted) γνους(not quoted) πολυν(not quoted) χρονον(not quoted) θελειςυγιηςγενεσθαι

ab.note: preceding 3 words uncertain in Cyril's text

div.ab: εγωειμιηαμπελοςκαιυμειςτακληματα(not quoted) --remainder of verse is ALLUSION only -- o menwn en emoi kagw en autw outos ferei karpon polun oti cwris emou ou dunasqe poiein ouden
(not quoted) εανγαρεγωμηαπελθωοπαρακλητοςουμηελθηερχεταιπρος(not quoted) umas ean de poreuqw pemyw auton pros umas --these words appear in one Cyril ms only
(not quoted) πιλατος(not quoted) καθεζομενος(not quoted) λιθοστρωτον(allusions)γολγοθαν
οιστρατιωται(not quoted) τεσσαρα(not quoted) οδεχιτων(not quoted)
